meta.description: Anti-pattern detection tips — shown when bad practices are detected

tips.tip: ⚠️ You have 5+ parallel worktrees. Start with 2-3 agents — more agents means more merge conflicts and harder reviews.
tips.fix: Close some worktrees. Focus on 2-3 parallel streams you can effectively review.

tips.tip: ⚠️ Your CLAUDE.md is over 200 lines. Move detailed workflows to Custom Commands — keep CLAUDE.md for core rules only.
tips.fix: Extract step-by-step workflows into .claude/commands/ files. CLAUDE.md should be a concise reference.

tips.tip: 💡 You have Custom Commands but no Hooks configured. Add a PostToolUse hook for type-checking to catch errors automatically.
tips.fix: Add to .claude/settings.json: a PostToolUse hook that runs your linter or type-checker after file edits.

tips.tip: ⚠️ AI-generated PRs must require human review. Add branch protection rules and use `needs-review` labels.
tips.fix: Enable branch protection: require 1+ review approvals. AI PRs should auto-add `ai-generated` and `needs-review` labels.

tips.tip: ⚠️ You have AI in CI/CD but no cost monitoring. Set billing alerts before costs surprise you.
tips.fix: Set up billing alerts at 50%, 80%, 100% of your monthly budget. Track cost per workflow type.

tips.tip: 💡 Your CLAUDE.md hasn't been updated in 30+ days. Stale instructions cause AI to generate outdated code patterns.
tips.fix: Review CLAUDE.md: does it still reflect your current tech stack, conventions, and directory structure?

tips.tip: 💡 Your test-to-source file ratio is very low. When delegating features to AI, always include 'with tests' in your prompt.
tips.fix: Add to your prompts: 'Include unit tests with >80% coverage for the new code.'
